title: Neck mass ENT documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting duration, fluctuation, infectious context, persistence, associated upper-aerodigestive features, and clinician concern in adults without asserting malignancy.
assessment.rationale: The exact adult neck-mass risk-history section supports onset, duration, progression, symptom impact, associated upper-aerodigestive features, relevant negatives, and clinician concern without asserting malignancy.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Evaluation of the Neck Mass in Adults
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE head-and-neck oncology, imaging, pathology, and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults older than 18 years with a neck mass; excludes children and masses known to originate from thyroid, salivary gland, mandibular, or dental pathology.
source.setting: Primary, dental, emergency, radiology, pathology, and ENT initial assessment of an adult neck mass.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult neck-mass duration and suspicious-history context, targeted head-and-neck examination, risk communication, documented follow-up, and education. It does not diagnose malignancy, order imaging or FNA, or generate referral.

exact_location.heading: Key action statements 1–2 — suspicious history, duration, and risk stratification

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting duration, fluctuation, infectious context, persistence, associated upper-aerodigestive features, and clinician concern in adults without asserting malignancy.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-performed examination of the oral cavity, oropharynx, laryngeal region, neck, and related mucosal sites when within competence; no finding is auto-populated.
assessment.rationale: The exact targeted-examination section supports clinician-performed ENT, oral, throat, and neck examination without auto-populating a mass or mucosal finding.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Evaluation of the Neck Mass in Adults
source.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE head-and-neck oncology, imaging, pathology, and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults older than 18 years with a neck mass; excludes children and masses known to originate from thyroid, salivary gland, mandibular, or dental pathology.

exact_location.heading: Key action statement 5 — targeted physical examination

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-performed examination of the oral cavity, oropharynx, laryngeal region, neck, and related mucosal sites when within competence; no finding is auto-populated.

assessment.text: Supports recording patient education, questions, planned follow-up, clinician concern, and already completed imaging or pathology context. Antibiotics, imaging, FNA, endoscopy, and referral decisions are not mapped.
assessment.rationale: The exact follow-up and education section supports concerns, reviewed specialist reports, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without ordering imaging, FNA, endoscopy, or referral.

assessment.text: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.
assessment.rationale: The exact NICE neck-lump and thyroid-lump recommendations were reviewed for age- and persistence-qualified cancer-recognition context; no automatic cancer or referral text was mapped.

source.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with symptoms or signs that may indicate cancer, subject to recommendation-specific age criteria.
source.setting: Primary care recognition, safety netting, investigation, and referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected suspicious skin-lesion, safety-netting, and age- or risk-qualified respiratory recommendations; international suspected-cancer pathways require UAE clinician review.
